issues.message: Repeated subexpression found (2x): ' Quantity], 'UDA'[Priority] = ' -- extract to a VAR
issues.fix: Use VAR to capture the repeated expression and reference the variable instead
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, the same subexpression is recalculated every time it appears, potentially doubling or tripling query cost.
requiredActions: Wrap the repeated subexpression in a VAR statement.
Reference the VAR in all places where the subexpression was used.

issues.message: Uses DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) which prevents single-pass aggregation
issues.fix: Use VAR to pre-compute CALCULATE result, then DIVIDE the variable
issues.whyItsBad: DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) forces the engine to evaluate the CALCULATE separately from the denominator, preventing single-pass aggregation. Each part generates its own DQ query.
requiredActions: Extract CALCULATE into a VAR variable.
Use DIVIDE(_var, denominator) in the RETURN clause.
This allows the engine to potentially combine the aggregations.

issues.message: Unqualified column references: [Retail Return Quantity], [Retail Sales Quantity] -- prefix with table name for clarity
issues.whyItsBad: Unqualified column names can resolve ambiguously when tables share column names.
requiredActions: Prefix all column references with 'TableName'[ColumnName].

issues.message: Measure has 4 lines of DAX but uses no VAR statements -- consider extracting intermediate calculations
issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
requiredActions: Introduce VAR statements for intermediate calculations.
Use RETURN to reference the final result.

issues.message: Hardcoded literals in filter context: = "REACT", = "REACT" -- consider parameterising
issues.fix: Extract hardcoded values to a parameter table or separate measure for maintainability
issues.whyItsBad: Hardcoded literals make measures fragile and hard to maintain. Changes require editing each measure individually.
requiredActions: Extract hardcoded values to a parameter table.
Reference the parameter table from the measure instead of literals.

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Contains 4 CALCULATE calls -- likely deeply nested, harming readability and performance
issues.fix: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
issues.whyItsBad: Contains 4 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
requiredActions: Flatten nested CALCULATE using VAR/RETURN pattern.
Use CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE(...)).
Extract inner calculations into separate reusable measures.

measures.name: Avg Weekly Retail Sales Value LW (Saleable RSV Cover)

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Contains 4 CALCULATE calls -- likely deeply nested, harming readability and performance
issues.fix: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
issues.whyItsBad: Contains 4 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
requiredActions: Flatten nested CALCULATE using VAR/RETURN pattern.
Use CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE(...)).
Extract inner calculations into separate reusable measures.

measures.name: Prime Tradeable Stock Buy Value Cover (Weeks) React

issues.message: Uses DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) which prevents single-pass aggregation
issues.fix: Use VAR to pre-compute CALCULATE result, then DIVIDE the variable
issues.whyItsBad: DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) forces the engine to evaluate the CALCULATE separately from the denominator, preventing single-pass aggregation. Each part generates its own DQ query.
requiredActions: Extract CALCULATE into a VAR variable.
Use DIVIDE(_var, denominator) in the RETURN clause.
This allows the engine to potentially combine the aggregations.

issues.message: Hardcoded literals in filter context: = "Test & React", = "React", = "Test" -- consider parameterising
issues.fix: Extract hardcoded values to a parameter table or separate measure for maintainability

requiredActions: Extract hardcoded values to a parameter table.
Reference the parameter table from the measure instead of literals.

measures.name: Reduced Tradeable Stock Buy Value Cover (Weeks) React

issues.message: Uses DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) which prevents single-pass aggregation
issues.fix: Use VAR to pre-compute CALCULATE result, then DIVIDE the variable
issues.whyItsBad: DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) forces the engine to evaluate the CALCULATE separately from the denominator, preventing single-pass aggregation. Each part generates its own DQ query.
requiredActions: Extract CALCULATE into a VAR variable.
Use DIVIDE(_var, denominator) in the RETURN clause.
This allows the engine to potentially combine the aggregations.

issues.message: Measure has 8 lines of DAX but uses no VAR statements -- consider extracting intermediate calculations
issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
requiredActions: Introduce VAR statements for intermediate calculations.
Use RETURN to reference the final result.

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Contains 7 CALCULATE calls -- likely deeply nested, harming readability and performance
issues.fix: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
issues.whyItsBad: Contains 7 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
requiredActions: Flatten nested CALCULATE using VAR/RETURN pattern.
Use CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE(...)).
Extract inner calculations into separate reusable measures.

issues.fix: Use VAR to capture the repeated expression and reference the variable instead
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, the same subexpression is recalculated every time it appears, potentially doubling or tripling query cost.
requiredActions: Wrap the repeated subexpression in a VAR statement.
Reference the VAR in all places where the subexpression was used.

issues.message: Unqualified column references: [Retail Sales Promotion Value Inc Tax], [Retail Sales Promotion Value Inc Tax LY] -- prefix with table name for clarity
issues.whyItsBad: Unqualified column names can resolve ambiguously when tables share column names.
requiredActions: Prefix all column references with 'TableName'[ColumnName].

issues.message: Measure has 5 lines of DAX but uses no VAR statements -- consider extracting intermediate calculations
issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
requiredActions: Introduce VAR statements for intermediate calculations.
Use RETURN to reference the final result.

issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Contains 7 CALCULATE calls -- likely deeply nested, harming readability and performance
issues.fix: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
issues.whyItsBad: Contains 7 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
requiredActions: Flatten nested CALCULATE using VAR/RETURN pattern.
Use CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE(...)).
Extract inner calculations into separate reusable measures.

measures.name: Tradeable Stock Buy Value Cover (Weeks) React

issues.message: Uses DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) which prevents single-pass aggregation
issues.fix: Use VAR to pre-compute CALCULATE result, then DIVIDE the variable
issues.whyItsBad: DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) forces the engine to evaluate the CALCULATE separately from the denominator, preventing single-pass aggregation. Each part generates its own DQ query.
requiredActions: Extract CALCULATE into a VAR variable.
Use DIVIDE(_var, denominator) in the RETURN clause.
This allows the engine to potentially combine the aggregations.

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, the same subexpression is recalculated every time it appears, potentially doubling or tripling query cost.
requiredActions: Wrap the repeated subexpression in a VAR statement.
Reference the VAR in all places where the subexpression was used.

issues.message: Measure has 4 lines of DAX but uses no VAR statements -- consider extracting intermediate calculations
issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
requiredActions: Introduce VAR statements for intermediate calculations.
Use RETURN to reference the final result.

issues.message: Contains 6 CALCULATE calls -- likely deeply nested, harming readability and performance
issues.fix: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
issues.whyItsBad: Contains 6 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
requiredActions: Flatten nested CALCULATE using VAR/RETURN pattern.
Use CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE(...)).
Extract inner calculations into separate reusable measures.

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Contains 3 CALCULATE calls -- likely deeply nested, harming readability and performance
issues.fix: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
issues.whyItsBad: Contains 3 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
requiredActions: Flatten nested CALCULATE using VAR/RETURN pattern.
Use CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE(...)).
Extract inner calculations into separate reusable measures.

issues.message: Measure has 4 lines of DAX but uses no VAR statements -- consider extracting intermediate calculations
issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
requiredActions: Introduce VAR statements for intermediate calculations.
Use RETURN to reference the final result.

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Measure has no formatString -- values may display with inconsistent formatting
issues.fix: Add a formatString (e.g. '#,0.00' for numbers, '0.0%' for percentages, '#,0' for integers)
issues.whyItsBad: Without an explicit format string, PBI uses raw floating-point display, causing inconsistencies across visuals.
requiredActions: Add formatString property (e.g. '#,##0.00' for currency, '0.0%' for percentages).

issues.whyItsBad: Without an explicit format string, PBI uses raw floating-point display, causing inconsistencies across visuals.
requiredActions: Add formatString property (e.g. '#,##0.00' for currency, '0.0%' for percentages).

issues.message: Measure has 4 lines of DAX but uses no VAR statements -- consider extracting intermediate calculations
issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
requiredActions: Introduce VAR statements for intermediate calculations.
Use RETURN to reference the final result.

issues.message: Repeated subexpression found (2x): ' [Retail Return Rate Value LW ' -- extract to a VAR
issues.fix: Use VAR to capture the repeated expression and reference the variable instead
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, the same subexpression is recalculated every time it appears, potentially doubling or tripling query cost.
requiredActions: Wrap the repeated subexpression in a VAR statement.
Reference the VAR in all places where the subexpression was used.

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

requiredActions: Prefix all column references with 'TableName'[ColumnName].

issues.message: Uses FILTER(ALL(...)) -- should use REMOVEFILTERS + direct predicate
issues.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
issues.whyItsBad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
requiredActions: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Col]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Col]) + direct predicate.
Or use KEEPFILTERS for AND filter semantics.
Test functional equivalence in DAX Studio with Server Timings.

issues.message: Contains 3 CALCULATE calls -- likely deeply nested, harming readability and performance
issues.fix: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
issues.whyItsBad: Contains 3 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
requiredActions: Flatten nested CALCULATE using VAR/RETURN pattern.
Use CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE(...)).
Extract inner calculations into separate reusable measures.

issues.message: Measure has 7 lines of DAX but uses no VAR statements -- consider extracting intermediate calculations
issues.fix: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
issues.whyItsBad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
requiredActions: Introduce VAR statements for intermediate calculations.
Use RETURN to reference the final result.

issues.message: Measure has no formatString -- values may display with inconsistent formatting
issues.fix: Add a formatString (e.g. '#,0.00' for numbers, '0.0%' for percentages, '#,0' for integers)
issues.whyItsBad: Without an explicit format string, PBI uses raw floating-point display, causing inconsistencies across visuals.
requiredActions: Add formatString property (e.g. '#,##0.00' for currency, '0.0%' for percentages).
